corrected_fields.why_it_matters: Reference rates produced by the NY Fed form the backbone of trillions of dollars in financial contracts and monetary policy implementation. SOFR has become the primary replacement for LIBOR in many markets. Understanding these rates is essential for evaluating money market dynamics and the transmission of monetary policy to broader financial markets.
corrected_fields.market_structure_context: This page describes foundational market infrastructure for reference rates. The EFFR reflects unsecured overnight federal funds transactions, OBFR adds Eurodollar transactions and deposits, SOFR measures the cost of financing Treasury securities overnight (broadest collateralized measure), BGCR covers Treasury general collateral repo, and TGCR covers tri-party Treasury GC repo. These rates collectively inform the pricing of trillions in contracts and serve as benchmarks for evaluating monetary policy transmission.
